Heirloom-quality furniture and custom woodwork, built one piece at a time in the Appalachian foothills.

View the Work

Selected Work

Built to Last

Every piece starts with the right board and ends with the right finish. Here's a look at some of Ray's recent commissions and personal projects.

Seating

Slatted Indoor Bench

A wide, welcoming bench in solid cherry with a slatted seat, gently curved back, and rounded arms with flat rests. An open lower shelf keeps baskets and books within reach. Built to be sat in every day for the next fifty years.

Bedroom

Oak Pedestal Side Table

A slim T-base side table in white oak — clean enough to tuck beside a bed, sturdy enough to last decades. The thick top and routed base give it quiet character without fuss.

Entryway

Hall Console Table

A long, slender console with trestle-style legs and a smooth, hand-finished top in warm cherry. Slim enough for a hallway, sturdy enough to hold whatever life brings through the front door.

Home Office

Mission Oak Writing Desk

A Craftsman-style writing desk in red oak with vertical slat sides and a broad, solid top. Clean lines, honest joinery, and a warm natural finish that only gets better with use.

Built-In

Built-In Bookcase & Cabinet Wall Unit

A floor-to-ceiling built-in wall unit in dark walnut with open shelving above and raised-panel cabinet doors below. The kind of piece that makes a room feel finished and permanent.

Storage

Media & Book Shelf Unit

A sturdy multi-shelf unit built to hold everything from a music collection to favorite novels. Solid construction with a painted finish and plenty of room to grow into.

Kitchen

Custom Oak Kitchen Cabinets

A full kitchen cabinet suite in solid red oak with raised-panel doors, a leaded-glass display cabinet, and a warm honey finish. Built and installed to fit the room exactly.

Entryway

Live-Edge Entryway Table

A rustic entryway table with a live-edge slab top and two contrasting walnut shelves below. The natural edge is left intact, letting the wood's own character lead the design.

About Ray

A Lifetime in the Shop

Ray Griffith was raised in Van Lear, Kentucky, and has called Morehead home for 57 years. Over that time he's woven himself into the fabric of the community in more ways than one — from running several local restaurants, including an early Kentucky Fried Chicken franchise, to spending the next 40 years serving Morehead's building and construction needs at Big 4 Lumber, now an Ace Hardware store.

Ray retired in the spring of 2026 and finally turned his full attention to the shop. He takes on a small number of woodworking projects each year, giving every piece the unhurried care it deserves. When he's not at the workbench, you'll find him with his grandchildren, deep in a good mystery novel, or spending time with his wife Nadine.
